user_query:
Build a reproducible multi-engine search URL kit for my research team. Read input/spec.json for 4 search briefs and follow these exact rules:
- For each brief, construct URLs for Google, DuckDuckGo (HTML endpoint), Brave, and Startpage using the query text and any required time filters or engine-specific parameters indicated in the brief.
- Always URL-encode the query parameter value with UTF-8 (encode spaces and special characters like ":"), then append engine parameters. Use these base endpoints exactly:
  • Google: https://www.google.com/search?q=
  • DuckDuckGo (HTML): https://duckduckgo.com/html/?q=
  • Brave: https://search.brave.com/search?q=
  • Startpage: https://www.startpage.com/sp/search?query=
- Apply time filters exactly when specified:
  • Google: week -> &tbs=qdr:w, year -> &tbs=qdr:y
  • Brave: week -> &tf=pw, year -> &tf=py
  • Startpage: week -> &time=week, year -> &time=year
- If a brief has type "compute", also generate a WolframAlpha URL at https://www.wolframalpha.com/input?i= with the same URL-encoded query.
- If a brief includes duckduckgo_params, append them to the DuckDuckGo URL in this exact order immediately after the q parameter: first &kp=..., then &kl=....
- Do not include extra parameters beyond those specified.

Write the outputs to:
- output/search_urls.json containing: { "briefs": [ { "id": string, "google": string, "duckduckgo": string, "brave": string, "startpage": string, "wolframalpha": string? } ] }
- output/README.md with a short summary listing each brief id and the engines provided for it.

Do not hardcode absolute paths; use the input/ and output/ folders exactly as above.
